candidate_llm_application: Nitinol-phase weight memory with deployment-time T-controlled snap + hysteresis deadband + two-way training

results.rationale: Source-domain Wikipedia page on nitinol — has source-domain keywords (nitinol, austenite/martensite) but zero LLM-side mechanism. Functionally orthogonal to LLM adapters. However, substring keyword overlap from source-domain content words triggers kw>=2.
Source-domain Wikipedia page on shape-memory alloys — discusses two-way thermal training and hysteresis. Source-domain only, no LLM mechanism, but substring keyword overlap is high.
Source-domain medical actuator blog. No LLM-side mechanism. Limited keyword overlap.

LoRA-Switch: dynamic per-token adapter switching at runtime. Operationally implements the core phase-snap (switch between adapter states at runtime), although without the explicit hysteresis-deadband or the two-way-training discipline. Functional equivalence >= 0.7.

Magic-Token Co-Training for Switchable Safety: explicit control-token toggles between divergent safety behaviors; co-training imprints both behaviors simultaneously, paralleling two-way training that imprints both shapes. Operationally matches T-controlled phase snap with dual-shape behaviors.

AdaptiveGuard: OOD-aware runtime safety with LoRA updates post-deployment. Runtime risk signal triggers a safety LoRA application = T-controlled snap leg of the candidate. Functional equivalence above threshold.
Fusion of LoRA for safety — combines adapters statically, not a runtime phase-snap with dual-state hysteresis. Below all thresholds.
LoRA single-direction safety alignment — describes a static low-rank safety subspace, not deployment-time T-controlled snap nor hysteresis. Below thresholds.

summary.rationale: Total hits >= 1 under FROZEN OR rule. Source-domain Wikipedia pages (ranks 1 and 2) trigger kw>=2 due to direct substring overlap of source-domain content words (nitinol, austenite martensite, thermal hysteresis loop, two-way training). Three LLM-side adapter-switching papers (ranks 4, 5, 6) cross func>=0.7, with rank 5 (Magic-Token Co-Training) being the strongest functional match — switchable safety control with co-training imprinting both behaviors directly mirrors the candidate's dual-shape T-controlled phase snap with two-way training discipline.
